title: Rabeprazole: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text: esomeprazole (Nexium, in Vimovo), lansoprazole (Prevacid), omeprazole (Prilosec, in Talicia, in Zegerid), pantoprazole (Protonix), any other medications, or any of the ingredients in rabeprazole tablets. Ask your pharmacist for a list of the ingredients. Some medications should not be taken with rabeprazole. Other medications may cause dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with rabeprazole. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting rabeprazole with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking rabeprazole, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist. The following nonprescription or herbal products may interact with rabeprazole: iron supplements. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking rabeprazole. Do not start any of these medications while taking rabeprazole without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have or have ever had a low level of magnesium, calcium, or potassium in your blood; hypoparathyroidism (condition in which the body does not produce enough parathyroid hormone [PTH; a natural substance needed to control the amount of calcium in the blood]); low levels of vitamin B12 in your body; osteoporosis (a condition in which the bones become thin and
